en.extract:
The black-headed penduline tit (Remiz macronyx) is a species of bird in the family Remizidae. It is found in Central Asia in reed beds along lakes or rivers. Its distribution is fragmented. It is the least thoroughly-documented bird in the Remiz genus, and has been described as one of the most poorly-known birds in Central Asia.


Diet
It is omnivorous.


Taxonomy
Four subspecies are recognised:

Remiz macronyx macronyx (Severtsov, 1873) – southwest Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, north & southeast Turkmenistan, Tajikistan, and northeast Afghanistan.
Remiz macronyx neglectus (Zarudny, 1908) – north Iran and south Turkmenistan. Its males typically have heads more fully covered in black plumage, with fewer traces of the chestnut seen in other subspecies.
Remiz macronyx nigricans (Zarudny, 1908) – southeast Iran and southwest Afghanistan. It is generally believed to have gone extinct. It had a dark head and a chestnut body.
Remiz macronyx ssaposhnikowi (Johansen, HE, 1907) – southeast Kazakhstan
The IUCN considers R. macronyx to be a synonym of Remiz pendulinus, the Eurasian penduline tit.


References

The '''black-headed penduline tit''' ('''''Remiz macronyx''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Remizidae]]. It is found in [[Central Asia]] in [[Reed bed|reed beds]] along [[Lake|lakes]] or [[River|rivers]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Wang |first1=Hui |last2=Mei |first2=Yu |last3=Que |first3=Pinjia |last4=Zhang |first4=Zhengwang |last5=Chen |first5=De |date=May 2023 |title=First breeding record of the black-headed penduline tit (''Remiz macronyx'') in China |journal=Ecology and Evolution |volume=13 |issue=5 |article-number=e10078 |doi=10.1002/ece3.10078 |pmid=37214619 |pmc=10199454 |bibcode=2023EcoEv..1310078W |issn=2045-7758}}</ref> Its distribution is fragmented.<ref name=":0" /> It is the least thoroughly-documented bird in the ''[[Remiz]]'' genus,<ref name=":0" /> and has been described as one of the most poorly-known birds in Central Asia.<ref name=":1">{{Cite journal |last1=Kittelberger |first1=Kyle D. |last2=Hohenthal |first2=Noel |last3=Ağırkaya |first3=Kayahan |last4=Çoban |first4=Ayşegül |last5=Hakkı Şekercioğlu |first5=Çağan |date=2022-10-02 |title=Poorly studied Black-headed Penduline Tit (''Remiz macronyx'') recorded for the first time in Turkey (Aves: Remizidae) |journal=Zoology in the Middle East |volume=68 |issue=4 |pages=283–290 |doi=10.1080/09397140.2022.2145796 |issn=0939-7140}}</ref>

== Diet ==
It is omnivorous.<ref>Bot, S., & van Dijk, R. E. (2009). Black-headed Penduline Tits Remiz macronyx in Kazakhstan. Sandgrouse, 31, 171–176</ref>

== Taxonomy ==
Four [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen | year=2023 | title=Waxwings and allies, tits, penduline tits | work=IOC World Bird List |version= 13.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/waxwings/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=8 December 2023}}</ref>
* ''Remiz macronyx macronyx'' (Severtsov, 1873) – southwest Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, north & southeast Turkmenistan, Tajikistan, and northeast Afghanistan.
* ''Remiz macronyx neglectus'' (Zarudny, 1908) – north Iran and south Turkmenistan. Its males typically have heads more fully covered in black plumage, with fewer traces of the chestnut seen in other subspecies.<ref name=":1" />
* ''Remiz macronyx nigricans'' (Zarudny, 1908) – southeast Iran and southwest Afghanistan. It is generally believed to have gone extinct.<ref>Shirihai, H., & Svensson, L. (2018). Handbook of Western Palearctic Birds, Volume 2: Passerines: Flycatchers to Buntings (1st ed.). London: Bloomsbury Publishing.</ref> It had a dark head and a chestnut body.<ref name=":1" />
* ''Remiz macronyx ssaposhnikowi'' (Johansen, HE, 1907) – southeast Kazakhstan

The [[IUCN]] considers ''R. macronyx'' to be a synonym of ''Remiz pendulinus'', the [[Eurasian penduline tit]].<ref name=IUCN/>

zh.extract:
黑头攀雀（学名：Remiz macronyx），是雀形目攀雀科的一种鸟类。


特征
黑头攀雀体重约9.3克，翼长约56.1毫米，嘴峰长约10.6毫米，喙宽度约3.1毫米，喙厚度约3.9毫米，跗蹠长约13.7毫米，尾长约48.5毫米。黑头攀雀是候鸟，其栖息在半开放的湖泊、沼泽等湿地环境中，食性为杂食性，主要食物来源是陆生无脊椎动物。


亚种
R. m. macronyx：分布于哈萨克斯坦西南部至乌兹别克斯坦、塔吉克斯坦和土库曼斯坦东南部。
R. m. neglectus：分布于土库曼斯坦西南部和伊朗北部（阿特拉克和戈尔甘山谷）。
R. m. nigricans：分布于伊朗东南部。
R. m. ssaposhnikowi：分布于哈萨克斯坦东南部（巴尔喀什湖、萨瑟克湖和阿拉湖的区域）。
